perturbation.original_text: 4.5 Personnel; Representative Director. One of the Directors to be nominated by AFSL shall be the manager of the Company, who shall also be the Representative Director (as defined under the Japanese Companies Act).
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5 Workforce; Executive Manager. One of the Directors to be nominated by AFSL shall be the manager of the Company, who may also be the Executive Manager (as potentially considered by the Japanese Companies Act).
perturbation.explanation: The term "Personnel" is substituted with "Workforce," and "Representative Director" is replaced with "Executive Manager," with softened language indicating uncertainty about the applicability of the Japanese Companies Act. This creates ambiguity around the role and its legal standing, directly contradicting the original definitive statement. It introduces uncertainty regarding the responsibilities and authority associated with the role, and if it would entail the full spectrum of legal obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Board. Except where the approval of the Shareholders is required by applicable Laws, the Articles of Incorporation or this Agreement, the business and affairs of the Company shall be managed by the Board.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Representative Director' with 'Executive Manager' and introduces uncertainty about the role's definition under the Japanese Companies Act. This contradicts section 4.1, which states that 'the business and affairs of the Company shall be managed by the Board.' The original text clearly defined the Representative Director's role, while the changed text creates ambiguity regarding the authority and responsibility associated with the 'Executive Manager' role and undermines the Board's management function.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 GaN Equipment. During the term of this Agreement, TPH-A/TPH shall be responsible for the costs and expenses agreed by the Parties to maintain and/or procure the equipment for wafer processing specified in the Appendix 2 attached hereto and as amended from time to time upon the mutual agreement of the Parties (the "GaN Equipment"). At TPH's sole option, TPH or TPH-A may purchase the GaN Equipment by completion of payment of the purchase price or depreciation cost for such equipment as set forth in Appendix 2. In such case: (i) TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, shall acquire sole and exclusive title to the GaN Equipment, free and clear of all Encumbrances, and none of FSL, AFSL or the Company shall have any right, title or interest in such GaN Equipment, (ii) such GaN Equipment shall be clearly labeled as the property of TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, and (iii) FSL and AFSL shall cause to be assigned to TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, all licenses and warranties for such GaN Equipment and the software or firmware required to operate such GaN Equipment that are attached to, installed on, or embodied in such GaN Equipment as of the Effective Date. During the term if this Agreement, the GaN Equipment shall be used exclusively in GaN wafer processing, and shall not be used in the silicon wafer processing for AFSL's or FSL's current products. In the event that the Company needs to use the GaN Equipment to provide products or services to any customer other than TPH- A or TPH or their Affiliates prior to the Put Closing Date or the Call Closing Date, the Company shall reimburse TPH-A or TPH for the use of such equipment in the manner to be discussed in good faith and agreed upon by the Parties, which reimbursement may be offset against amounts otherwise owing from TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 GaN Machinery. During the term of this Agreement, TPH-A/TPH shall be responsible for the costs and expenses agreed by the Parties to maintain and/or procure some of the equipment for wafer processing, maybe, as specified in the Appendix 2 attached hereto and as it's amended from time to time maybe as well upon the mutual agreement of the Parties (the "GaN Equipment"). At TPH's sole option, TPH or TPH-A may purchase the GaN Machinery by completion of payment of the purchase price or depreciation cost for such machinery maybe as set forth in Appendix 2. In such case: (i) TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, shall maybe acquire sole and exclusive title to the GaN Machinery, probably free and clear of all Encumbrances, and potentially none of FSL, AFSL or the Company shall have any right, title or interest in such GaN Machinery, (ii) such GaN Machinery shall be labeled as the property of TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, and (iii) FSL and AFSL may cause to be assigned to TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, all licenses and warranties for such GaN Machinery and the software or firmware required to operate such GaN Machinery that are attached to, installed on, or embodied in such GaN Machinery as of the Effective Date. During the term if this Agreement, the GaN Machinery shall be used in GaN wafer processing, and shall not be used in the silicon wafer processing for AFSL's or FSL's current products. In the event that the Company may needs to use the GaN Machinery to provide products or services to any customer other than TPH- A or TPH or their Affiliates prior to the Put Closing Date or the Call Closing Date, the Company shall reimburse TPH-A or TPH for the use of such machinery in the manner to be discussed in good faith and agreed upon by the Parties, which reimbursement may be offset against amounts otherwise owing from TPH-A or TPH, as the case may be, to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The term "GaN Equipment" is intermittently replaced with "GaN Machinery", and softening language is used (e.g., "maybe", "potentially", "probably") and the equipment in the first line is not fully the responsible of TPH-A/TPH and some of the others equipment it's not clear if it will exist, injecting uncertainty about their obligations regarding the maintenance, procurement, and ownership of wafer processing equipment. This change contradicts the original text's intent, weakening the enforcement of specific equipment-related responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.7.21 creating, incurring, assuming or permitting to exist any indebtedness, except to the extent consistent with the then-current Business Plan;
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty and softens obligations regarding 'GaN Machinery', including procurement, ownership, and usage. The insertion of 'maybe,' 'potentially,' and 'probably' undermines the definiteness required for 'GaN Equipment' maintenance and obligations. This directly contradicts the commitment to avoiding indebtedness that does not align with the business plan, as specified in Section 4.7.21, by making unclear who is responsible for the equipment and how it should be maintained, potentially incurring unforeseen expenses inconsistent with the business plan.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 Conditions to Put Option or Call Option Closing. Each Party's obligation to complete the Put Option or Call Option at the Put Closing Date or Call Closing Date, as applicable, is subject to the fulfillment on or before such Put Closing Date or Call Closing Date of each of the following conditions, unless waived in writing (where permissible) by the applicable party in such closing:
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 Requirements to Put Option or Call Option finalization. Every actor's obligation to end the Put Option or Call Option at the Put Closing Date or Call Closing Date, depends in the fulfillment on or before such Put Closing Date or Call Closing Date of the next conditions, unless waved verbally (where permissible) by the applicable party in such finalization:
perturbation.explanation: "Conditions" is replaced with "Requirements", "complete" is swapped with "end", "Party's" is changed to "Every actor's", and a word of "subject" is swapped by "depends" and "waved verbally" instead of "waived in writing". It injects ambiguity around the enforceability and flexibility of the closing conditions, as a verbal wave may not entail that certain legaly aspect are being taking into consideration. It can result in disputes over the validity of the option exercises.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.9 Waiver. No action taken pursuant to this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute a waiver of compliance with any representation, warranty, covenant or agreement contained in this Agreement and shall not operate or be construed as a waiver of a similar or dissimilar nature. A Party may by written notice (a) extend the time for performance of any of the obligations or other actions of any other Parties under this Agreement, (b) waive any inaccuracies in the representations or warranties of any other shareholder contained in this Agreement, or (c) waive or modify performance of any of the covenants or obligations of any other Parties under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces 'waived in writing' with 'waved verbally'. The contradicted text, Section 11.9, explicitly requires waivers to be provided via 'written notice'. Thus, the modified text directly contradicts the requirement of written waivers, creating a clear in-text contradiction related to waiver procedures.
